column35: Comments
Ultron really helped take care of his alternate reality version. He help prevent many wounds. Side 2 sadly lost much vp due to schemes.

My first attempt was terrible. I also chose a 'wrong' hero, Star-Lord T'Challa for that attempt, which would make all the empowered stuff harder (Sentries and Ultron, Infinty). The city felt ok, I had recruited multiple Strength cards from Party Thor to kill Gammenon soon and some Ultron Sentries to defeat, but he managed to escape pretty early and was followed by Tiamut in turn 10. I just gave up at that point, as it went rough, and I can't imagine recovering from that.

Attempt 2, I changed my heroes first. Gamora, with in mind that I might need +Infinity attack if the game takes longer. And Carnage for more Instinct to hopefully preventg Tiamut escaping again. I ended up drawing lots of Ultron Sentries, and only a few Celestials and Strange's Demons. The Ultron Sentries helped KO for Deck 1 greatly. I ended up with 9 starters KO'd. Also, the Celestials I drew were double Gammenon and one Nezzar. I had been building deck 1 for Strength and Tech cards, with mostly Venom's 4-costs, Killmonger's 4-costs, Carnage's 4-costs and 1 of Killmonger's 2 costs. I also recruited 1 Madame Hydra once and a 2-cost of Doctor Strange Supreme. If I recall correctly in turn 11 I could defeat the Double Gammenon and two other Ultron Sentries. I had a stroke of good luck with the triggers and both of Killmonger's 4-costs and 4 S.H.I.E.L.D. Troopers (I had then already KO'd 5 of my S.H.I.E.L.D. Agents) preceded by Carnage's 4 cost and Killmonger's 2-cost for 14 attack. Both Gammenon's were only 4 attack due to Cosmic Threat. So I defeated them both and then the 2 Ultron Sentries and rescued 5 bystanders that turn. Nezzar was defeated earlier by deck 2. Turn 13 was another villain and two more bystanders and I recruited another of Carnage's 4-costs. Turn 15 was the magical turn in which I ended up buying Gamora's 4-cost, "Wield the Blade of Thanos" and defeated the Strange's Demon that let you draw two cards (for Soulbinding) to draw my discard pile with Madame Hydra and Gamora's 4-cost (I gambled that it would be worth it as I had already drawn 8 cards that turn, 4 from double Carnage's 4-cost, 1 from Doctor Strange Supreme and 1 from Dodging Madame Hydra. I didn't see the infinite combo until those two cards were in my hand though. I dodged the Madame Hydra to draw itself over 9000 times and then played Gamora's 4-cost for a win in turn 15!

Deck 2 tried to build into Gamora and Doctor Strange, but was severly underimpressive. And perhaps luckily so. Deck 1 could really shine because almost al the Sentries showed up and almost all got defeated by deck 1. Deck 2 in total defeated 3 enemies. One Ultron Sentry, one Nezzar and one Strange's Demon (for the 2 KO's from discard pile for double Soulbind (which I used).

Gamenon the gather giving bystanders per villain defeated, Ronan giving shards per bystanders, ronan using said shards to draw more ronans and killmongers for green/tech synergies, giving villains more bystanders and activating liberate to get more bystanders and more shards and more cards! Oh and the prizes from contest of champions? Thats right, more bystanders and shards! Mysterio made great use of Venom . . . who was never bought but used to go to the bottom of the deck since shifting decoy could get +2 in fight and recruit from every venom card, unless I really needed to move a certain colour out of hq to fight ultron I could possibly gamble on changing empowerment numbers. Great setup with challenging decision making of whe to soulbound and when to keep boons.

Infinity Ultron is always challenging, but I felt good about my Hero(?) choices this round. Killmonger and Strange are excellent anchors to decks, and I went with Ultron to pair with Killmonger to generate massive attack, and Electro with Strange to potentially generate infinite attack with Supercharge in the event Ultron went infinite himself. That wasn't needed as only 4 Strikes showed up, and Killmonger and Ultron (the normal one) were enough to take down 5 Celestials and Ultron (the Infinity one) 3 times.

Had a tough choice for heroes here. I knew I wanted to use Ultron as it was fun to have him against himself. Other than that it was really between Gamora, Black Widow and Green goblin with also the outside possibility of Electro. Gamora is just strong in any setup and her rare would allow hitting Ultron when he had all the stones but I felt if the game had got to that point I had taken too many turns anyway. Went with Goblin in the end so hope I made the right call.

That was a really disappointing set up to be faced with for the first elimination round. I feel we had absolutely no chance either game as the villain deck ensured we couldn't hit Ultron. If we go out here, it'll leave a bad taste in my mouth.
As for the game, Ultron went infinite on turn 18 in game 1, then in game 2 we lasted until the penultimate turn before he was unbeatable. I had added Gamora as a last ditch attempt at hitting him on infinite, but I never had more than one villain in my victory pile during the entire game. My wife collected a fair few Celestials for points, but we were forced to focus on the City we never got close to hitting Ultron.

Difficult. I couldn't get the right balance between managing the city and getting to Ultron in time. Before I knew it, he got 5 Master Strikes right away and my game was basically over. Was hoping the Gamora rare could save me from it but never drew it in two games.
We'll see if I picked the right attempt.  Hardest choice of the round.

column35: One of the best Iceman/Bishop games in a while. I also got Moondragon's Danger Sense to keep pushing away strikes and twists and surfacing juicy celestials. Final turn ended up with 64 attack ^^

I played first game and had an AMAZING SCORE! thought it was too good to be true so read more in depth about the celestial boon from “nezarr, the calculator”. It just seemed TOO OP to reduce mastermind strength to 1/5th power. Indeed. I read it wrong. Didn’t see the minus sign before the attack symbol (reduce attack by 1/5th rounded down). So I chucked that score out and played correctly the best round! lol. Still thought we did pretty well.
Wolverine did not help as much as we thought, but Red Guardian did his thing, recruited all red cards, and gave us 2 big turns. We lost game 2.
Another setup where the melk wasn’t flowing

column35: This was deckbuilding at its finest, finding 3 heroes who just clicked together. Maximus made whole setup look easy with his common, Kitty was trashing wounds on one side, and managing HQ on the other, and Red Guardian just went "Red deck wins" (my favorite deck from ancient times I played MTG)

Red Guardian rocks!!! Brought Maximus to both support Red Guardian and defeat the "trained" henchmen. Brought Captain Mar-Vell to support Moondragon and for extra danger sense, as I wanted to delay the twists/strikes as much as possible. Covert side ended up hitting both copies of Nezar for "free" resulting in a Taskmaster with a base attack of 3.

I accidentally cheated my first round, didn't notice the "give wounds" part of taskmaster until turn 19. I forfeited that one and submitted my second.

Yondu felt really good going against this setup. I also think I found a new favorite villain group in the Celestials!

column35:
Scored Medusa's rare at turn six, due to it being the only odd card in the HQ when Witchfire came off the villain deck.

Brought Karnak for no reason, other than to sit in the HQ and nerf Abomination bonuses. M was able to support both the Stepford Cuckoos side and the Medusa/card draw side.

First play went pretty smoothly, but one or two things I thought maybe I could do better, so let's see how I can improve.  Nope.  Second play was more turns for fewer points and more escapes.  Turns out, those Sinister Clones went easy on me first time around and I forgot what a pain they can be flooding the City early.
Maximus is a jerk and always used the wrong Master Strike at the wrong time, everytime!

Stepford Cuckoos carried this for me. I only won this attempt and took an awful long time to do it, with 2 escapes, so I don’t expect to rank high. Tried using Karnak on the other attempt to get When Recruited bonuses and take out Sinister Clones before they could escape, but it did not work!
A setup worthy of Keyword Overload. Maximus was a good bit tougher than I remember him being.

This was a fun puzzle. I ended up going with Warlock and Black Cat in the second game (wouldn’t usually pick them) because they had lots of cards without printed strike to try and sabotage the abominations. It worked well, but the game took a long time and Mr. sinister henchmen forced some escapes (showed up on turn 3, 5, 8) because I had 4 recruit/2 strike in both hands for the first rounds.
Glad to see some new cards I haven’t played much with!
Couldn't ramp up fast enough.

I did not enjoy this set up. I felt like I jits never could get enough buy and then had a string of horrible luck in both lf my games that I ended up getting beat by the scheme while there was still plenty of deck left. I even played it a third time just to try different heros but not count it and ran into the same issue. I'm interested to see how others faired and who they took.

Really fun setup. A scheme/mastermind pair thats very fair but still challenging, and the available sets made hero selection fun as there were so many valid strategies.

Turns out though, I didnt need any strategy in my games.  It was trucks.  Just lots and lots of trucks hurled at a high velocity, right to Mlkeith's face..

We did really bad on the first game, lots of Scheme twists early, had 3 8 cost cards filling the HQ. just did not go our way. We were able to get the mastermind hit quickly the second game and the cards just were on our side. Not a terribly hard combo of Mastermind and Scheme.

I decided to go all-in on recruit power and try to defeat the enemies with "bribe" or by putting them to the Astral plane. Joe Fixit and The Ancient One were really good choices for that strategy, while She-Hulk helps the high recruit setups. Doctor Strange does nice combos with his own cards, so he was a good support also. I just decided to mostly ignore Multiple Man. In the first game my hero recruits were really strong from the beginning, finished the game pretty quickly (25 turns), but I let 3 villains escape. The second game was a little longer, but I had only 1 escape and a better PPT score. I just didn't see any rare cards in that game :( Combining the hero deck luck from my first game with the villain deck luck from the second would result in a really short game :D But overall I really did enjoy both of my games, 5/5 =)

column35:
Some of my favorites are in this setup. 
I paired Amadeus Cho with multiple man and the deck ended up being a really solid choice. I outwitted every turn given MMs cards are all four and Amadeus’s cards aren’t. So any given hand with both of them and a starting card hit the outwit. And the instinct tech pairing between them got me 13/14 of MMs cards so I drew a ton and got stranges rare and Amadeus’s rare. Got to use them both too, I cycled the deck so quickly. Also I KOed my every starting card on the left thanks to MM rare and feasting smartly.
Right hand went the artifact route with strange and sif. Drew a consistent attack amount. Not a ton dependable. Also KOed 7 starters thanks to feasting. 
I drew three scheme twists back to back thanks to the scheme mechanics and I made a lot of use of the time, given I also drew four rates back to back into the hq. But korg KOed one and once the Me, Myself, and I machine got going I recruited the Eye of Agamotto and Visualize the Variables, the Re-Absorb Duplicates wasn’t too hard to get either. 
Multiple Man is one of my favorite heroes to use. If he gets into the HQ even a little bit he has been so dependable for me. And I love doing different kinds of artifacts from different sets. so this game was so well designed and super fun to play! Thank you!

column35: First off, I always appreciate the punny names of the setups and I especially liked this ones pun since I'm a big Nightmare on Elm Street fan. This was definitely a game of firsts for me. First time playing with villainous weapons, astral plane1, and transforming cards. I used three transforming herpes and managed to trigger a transformation upgrade once for each of them. I'm really glad I had such heavy hitters since the villainous weapons really stacked up. I didn't worry too much since I managed the city pretty well which enhanced the fun factor for me. This setup is definitely worth playing!
Nil

Enjoyed the setup. Never quite the Black Widow bystander machine engine going in either game, but drawing lots of cards and staying on tech was helpful. 
Btw, I keep having issues with my scores the last few weeks. The results have been showing a lot more escapes than I've had or entered in the system. For the record, I had 0 escapes for the game I'm posting. That is also what I see on the form as I submit it.

column35: Pretty basic setup. Those scheme twists are dangerous though.

Tech cards and dual-symbol synergies! My choices of Domino and Forge felt great to pair hyperspeed and versatile nicely. Readily satisfied tech card requirements, including a relatively early recruited Forge B.F.G. which consistently was huge! Interesting potential tension between Nick Fury's opportunity to recruit Shield Officers while the Scheme didn't want you to run out of them. Ended up not being too relevant though. I took the opportunity to fight the Scheme's Officers rather than recruit them, so my officer deck wasn't in danger of running out. Interesting setup with different interactions!

I thought a hyper speed deck with domino and Darkhawk would be fun, my wife did not enjoy it. Game 1 we got 5 twists in the first 7 turns, spent too much time stressing the scheme that by the time we got it handled it was too late and we had ignored the MM. Game 2 was a little better but it took a while to get to hit the MM. Master Strike keep discarding my Storm rare and I never got to use it despite buying it on turn 8.
Hyperspeed never felt so good

Deceptively hard. One of our worst tournament submissions ever. First game we lost to the scheme in 27 turns so the second time tried to focus really hard on saving Officers but never got to attack the Hood once. Maybe would have been better served to try two games completely ignoring the scheme and going for the MM.

I enjoyed how Forge and War Machine worked with the other heroes but Hood was extremely disruptive and with all the KO's from the scheme it was more likely he dumped 4-5 cards every time.  Lost with 9 attack in hand and all tactics gone.

column35:
With the stress for wanting to perform well now gone, I aimed to have a bit thematic, fun game. So against Sinister and phalanx I picked Jean Grey and Cable (X-Men 97 motivations). Also I felt that Jean will be fun with the many bystanders. 
I did not expect, that I will be quite lucky. Left-hand could pick good JG cards early, and getting the Rare in turn 7. Other hand went for Colossus, then Cable. 
With Jean Cards in place, and actually useful KOs coming from Feast and Phalanx. Left-hand started steamrolling Sinister. (1 tactic down in turn 9, 1 in turn 13, 2 in turn 15). Reshuffles were lucky and Vertigo villain also helped. By turn 15 I also had the card draw/ bystander Jean Card, so ended up having all my deck to deal 2 blows to Sinister, and clear the city as well.  In the meantime, due to some well timed master strikes and three copies of Cable's  Disaster survavibility I ended up drawing 9 extra cards, also having the whole deck for Right-Hand. This way Cable could deliver the final blow. 
So this turned out to be one of those low turn count, albeit high VP games. (Too bad this did not happen on Week 4 :). 
I guess there was something about a bomb as well, and quite unexpected Quake and Groot cameos... 
So yeah, just wanted to have fun and actually had it!
First game: did not see iceman until the end green carried me to victory. Second game iceman finally showed up and took me to victory but much slower

Spider-Woman gave me JUST enough deck manipulation that I could mitigate some of the risks associated with Feast in this scheme, and Deathlok gave me SHIELD levels for Quake while alsos providing me with Tech to avoid Phalanx KOs. BOTH of my games had the first two scheme twists IMMEDIATELY blow up on me.

column35:
I decided to go heavy S.H.I.E.L.D. to see if Undercover could work for deck thinning, since there wasn't a lot of KO in the setup. I think it worked reasonably well. There were several cards that let me draw an extra card or two, which protected me from Master Strikes a number of times. I also got reasonably lucky with Scheme Twists, both not drawing many early and only having one actually wipe the HQ. The one Villain escape probably doomed this attempt to the bottom half of the pod, but I'm fighting off a cold and didn't feel up to a second playthrough.

Player 2 went almost mono-green, and got several of Groot's cards that let me KO cards, so by the end of the game I had literally no starters left and was basically playing my whole deck every turn. So that was fun.
